shortDescription: US Embassy B1/B2 · Tourism, family, business · 214(b) interview

policyAlert.summary: Confirm current B1/B2 MRV fee (USD $185) on travel.state.gov before quoting clients.

alert.title: 214(b) presumption
alert.body: Every B1/B2 applicant must overcome immigrant intent presumption at interview. Ties to India and temporary purpose are decisive.

about.label: Description
about.value: B1 (business) and B2 (tourism/medical) combined visa for temporary visits to the United States. Indian nationals apply via DS-160 and mandatory consular interview.

about.label: Eligible applicants
about.value: Temporary visit purpose · Strong ties to India · Sufficient funds · Clean immigration history · Honest DS-160

about.label: Interview reality
about.value: Most decisions made in short interview. Officer assesses intent, ties, and credibility—not document volume alone.

about.label: Key authority
about.value: US Department of State — Embassy/Consulate in India

about.label: After approval
about.value: Visa foil in passport; officer at port of entry decides admission length (often up to 6 months). No work permitted.

redFlagsBanner: After 214(b) refusal, do not reapply immediately with unchanged profile. Strengthen ties evidence and visit purpose clarity first.

redFlags.title: Weak ties to India
redFlags.description: Unemployed, no assets, young single applicant with vague purpose.
redFlags.fix: Employment letter, business registration, property, family dependencies

redFlags.title: 214(b) — immigrant intent signals
redFlags.description: Mentions working in USA, long stay without reason, or visiting fiancé without clear plan.
redFlags.fix: Clear temporary purpose; return date; no employment intent

redFlags.title: DS-160 inconsistencies
redFlags.description: Travel history, employment, or relatives in US not matching interview.
redFlags.fix: Review DS-160 line by line; practice consistent answers

redFlags.title: Prior refusals unchanged
redFlags.description: Reapplying without material change in circumstances.
redFlags.fix: Wait until ties strengthen; new purpose documentation

redFlags.title: Insufficient funds
redFlags.description: Cannot explain who pays for trip or recent large deposits.
redFlags.fix: Sponsor ITR, bank history, trip budget breakdown

redFlags.title: Over-documented or fake papers
redFlags.description: Fabricated invitation or employment letters detected.

faqs.a: No. Consular officers decide at interview based on individual assessment.

faqs.q: What documents to carry to interview?
faqs.a: Passport, DS-160 confirmation, photo, appointment letter, plus supporting docs—employment, funds, invitation if applicable. Officer may not review all.
Demonstrate strong ties, clear temporary purpose, credible funds, and consistent honest answers—not memorised scripts.
Yes anytime, but success requires materially changed circumstances or stronger evidence.
No. Employment requires appropriate work visa. B1 allows limited business activities—not US labour market work.
Admission period set at port of entry—often up to 6 months. Must not overstay.
Not mandatory but helpful for family visits. Host's status proof strengthens file.
No. Visa validity differs from permitted stay per entry. Each entry is subject to CBP admission.

compliance: Client service agreement and consent on file before DS-160
Do not fabricate documents or coach false statements
Separate consultancy fee from MRV in quotes
proTips: Keep interview answers under 30 seconds—officer controls pace
Bring ties evidence even if officer doesn't ask
For family visits, know host's name, status, and address
Avoid mentioning immigrant petitions unless directly asked
Mock interview before appointment for nervous clients
postApproval: Explain visa validity vs admission period to client
Remind no work rule and I-94 compliance
Advise travel insurance and port-of-entry documents

changelog.author: Service Library
changelog.summary: Initial B1/B2 counselor content with 214(b) focus.

staffNotes.author: Documentation team
staffNotes.text: Clients visiting adult children in USA need clear return plan—property or business in India is critical.

staffNotes.author: Quality
staffNotes.text: Flag any client with pending immigrant petition before B1/B2 counseling.

resources.title: US State Dept — India visa information

quiz.question: Section 214(b) means:
options: Presumption of immigrant intent applicant must overcome
quiz.explanation: Core legal hurdle for non-immigrant visas.

quiz.question: Can B1/B2 holders work in USA?
options: Only remote for Indian employer without limit

quiz.question: After 214(b) refusal, best next step?
options: Strengthen ties and purpose before reapplying
quiz.explanation: Material change needed for reapplication success.

dos: Prepare strong ties evidence—employment, property, family
Explain trip purpose and funding clearly
Disclose relatives in USA honestly on DS-160
donts: Do not coach client to hide US relatives or prior refusals
Do not suggest working or long-term stay on visitor visa
Do not submit fabricated invitation letters
Do not reapply immediately after refusal without changes
mistakes: Unemployed applicant with no ties documentation
DS-160 travel history not matching passport
